1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: WHEN THE SKY is split asunder,

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: When the heaven shall be cleft,

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: When the heaven is cleft asunder.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: When the heaven is split asunder,

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: When the heaven is cleft asunder (Infatarat).

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: When the heaven is cleft asunder,

1.author: Qarai
1.text: When the sky is rent apart,

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: When the heavens are rent asunder,

1.author: Shakir
1.text: When the heaven becomes cleft asunder,

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: When the sky is cleft asunder;

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: When the Sky is cleft asunder;

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: And when the stars fall down.

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: And when the stars shall be scattered,

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: And when the stars have fallen and scattered;

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: And when the stars fall, scattering,

2.author: Shakir
2.text: And when the stars become dispersed,

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: and when the stars are scattered;

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: When the oceans begin to flow,

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: And when the oceans are swept away.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: And when the seas are flowed out,

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And when the seas are burst forth (got dried up);

3.author: Maududi
3.text: when the seas are made to burst forth,

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: When the seas are poured forth,

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: when the oceans are gushed forth,

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: And when the seas are erupted

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And when the seas are made to flow forth,

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: When the Oceans are suffered to burst forth;

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: And when the graves are overturned.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: And when the graves are ransacked,

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: And when the graves are turned upside down (and they bring out their contents)

4.author: Itani
4.text: When the tombs are strewn around.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: and when graves are laid open,

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: And when the [contents of] graves are scattered,

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: and the graves are turned inside out,

4.author: Shakir
4.text: And when the graves are laid open,

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: and when the graves are laid open:

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: And when the Graves are turned upside down;-

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Each soul will know what it had sent ahead and what it had left behind.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: Every soul will come to know what it has sent ahead and what it left behind.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: then a soul shall know its works, the former and the latter.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Each soul shall know that which it sent afore and that which it left behind.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: (Then) a person will know what he has sent forward and (what he has) left behind (of good or bad deeds).

5.author: Itani
5.text: Each soul will know what it has advanced, and what it has deferred.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: everyone shall know all his deeds, both the earlier and the later.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: A person will know what he has sent forward and left behind.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: A soul will know what it hath sent before (it) and what left behind.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: then a soul shall know what it has sent ahead and left behind.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: the soul shall know what it has done, the former and the latter.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: A soul will [then] know what it has put forth and kept back.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: every soul will see the result of its deeds - those recorded before his death and those which will produce either virtue or evil after his death.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Every soul shall know what it has sent before and held back.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: then everyone will know what he has sent ahead, and what he has left behind.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: (Then) shall each soul know what it hath sent forward and (what it hath) kept back.

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: O man, what seduced you from your munificent Lord

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: O man! What has deceived you away from your Lord, the Most Beneficent?

6.author: Arberry
6.text: O Man! What deceived thee as to thy generous Lord

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: O man! what hath beguiled thee concerning thy Lord, the Bountiful,

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: O man! What has made you careless concerning your Lord, the Most Generous?

6.author: Itani
6.text: O man! What deluded you concerning your Lord, the Most Generous?

6.author: Maududi
6.text: O man! What has deceived you about your generous Lord

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: O man! What has made you careless about your Lord, the Most Generous

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: O man! What hath made thee careless concerning thy Lord, the Bountiful,

6.author: Qarai
6.text: O man! What has deceived you about your generous Lord,

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: O human! What has deceived you concerning your Generous Lord

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: O mankind, what has deceived you concerning your Lord, the Generous,

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: Human being, what evil has deceived you about your Gracious Lord,

6.author: Shakir
6.text: O man! what has beguiled you from your Lord, the Gracious one,

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: O man! What is it that lures you away from your bountiful Sustainer,

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: O man! What has seduced thee from thy Lord Most Beneficent?-

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Ya ayyuha alinsanu ma gharraka birabbika alkareemi

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: Who created you then formed your symmetry, then gave you right proportion,

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: The One Who created you, then moulded you, then made you proper?

7.author: Arberry
7.text: who created thee and shaped thee and wrought thee in symmetry

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: Who created thee, then moulded thee, then proportioned thee?

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: Who created you, fashioned you perfectly, and gave you due proportion;

7.author: Itani
7.text: He Who created you, and formed you, and proportioned you?

7.author: Maududi
7.text: Who created you, shaped you, and made you well-proportioned,

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: Who created you, fashioned you perfectly, and gave you due proportion.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Who created thee, then fashioned, then proportioned thee?

7.author: Qarai
7.text: who created you and proportioned you, and gave you an upright nature,

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: who created you, formed you and proportioned you?

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: Who created you, proportioned you, and balanced you?

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: Who created you proportionately and fashioned you

7.author: Shakir
7.text: Who created you, then made you complete, then made you symmetrical?

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: who created you, fashioned you and proportioned you,

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Him Who created thee. Fashioned thee in due proportion, and gave thee a just bias;

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Shaping you into any form He pleased?

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: He moulded you into whatever shape He willed.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: and composed thee after what form He would?

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: In whatsoever form He listed He constructed thee.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: In whatever form He willed, He put you together.

8.author: Itani
8.text: In whatever shape He willed, He assembled you.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: and set you in whatever form He pleased?

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: In whatever form He willed, He put you together.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Into whatsoever form He will, He casteth thee.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: and composed you in any form that He wished?

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: In whatever shape He will He could surely have fashioned you.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: In whatever form He willed has He assembled you.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Into whatever form He pleased He constituted you.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: In whatever Form He wills, does He put thee together.

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Fee ayyi sooratin ma shaa rakkabaka

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: Even then you deny the Judgement.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: Not at all – but rather you deny the establishment of Justice.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: No indeed; but you cry lies to the Doom;

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: By no means! Aye, ye belie the the Requital.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Nay! But you deny the Recompense (reward for good deeds and punishment for evil deeds).

9.author: Maududi
9.text: No indeed; (the fact is that) you deny the Reckoning, declaring it a lie;

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: Nay! But you deny (the Day of) Ad-Din.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: Nay, but ye deny the Judgment.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: No! Indeed, you deny the Retribution.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: No! But you deny the Recompense.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: Despite this, you deny the Day of Judgment,

9.author: Shakir
9.text: Nay! but you give the lie to the judgment day,

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: Yet you deny the Last Judgement.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: Nay! But ye do reject Right and Judgment!

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: Surely there are guardians over you,

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And indeed there are some guardians over you.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: yet there are over you watchers

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Verify there are for you guardians,

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: But verily, over you (are appointed angels in charge of mankind) to watch you.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: you do so the while there are watchers over you;

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: But verily, over you to watch you

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Lo! there are above you guardians,

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Indeed, there are over you watchers,

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: Yet over you there are watchers,

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And indeed, [appointed] over you are keepers,

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: but you should know that there are angelic guards

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And most surely there are keepers over you

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: Surely, there are guardians watching over you,

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: But verily over you (are appointed angels) to protect you,-

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: Kind and honourable,- Writing down (your deeds):

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: Knowing all what you may do.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: They know all that you do.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: They know all that you do.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: who know of all that you do.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: and these honorable scribes know whatever you do.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: who know all that you do:

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: They know (and understand) all that ye do.

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: The pious will surely be in heaven,

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: Indeed the virtuous are in serenity.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: Surely the pious shall be in bliss,

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: Verily the pious shall be in Delight.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: Verily, the Abrar (pious and righteous) will be in delight (Paradise);

13.author: Itani
13.text: The virtuous will be in bliss.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: Surely the virtuous shall be in Bliss,

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: Verily, the Abrar (the righteous believers) will be in Delight;

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: Lo! the righteous verily will be in delight.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: Indeed the pious shall be amid bliss,

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: Indeed, the righteous shall (live) in bliss.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: Indeed, the righteous will be in pleasure,

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: The virtuous ones will live in bliss

13.author: Shakir
13.text: Most surely the righteous are in bliss,

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: the virtuous will dwell in bliss,

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: As for the Righteous, they will be in bliss;

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And indeed the sinners are in hell.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: and the libertines shall be in a fiery furnace

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And verily the ungodly shall be in a Scorch.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And verily, the Fujjar (the wicked, disbelievers, sinners and evil-doers) will be in the blazing Fire (Hell),

14.author: Itani
14.text: While the wicked will be in Hell.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: and the wicked shall be in the Blazing Fire.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And verily, the wicked will be in the blazing Fire (Hell),

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And lo! the wicked verily will be in hell;

14.author: Qarai
14.text: and indeed the vicious shall be in hell,

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: But the wicked, indeed they shall be in the Fiery Furnace,

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And indeed, the wicked will be in Hellfire.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: and the evil-doers will be in hell

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And most surely the wicked are in burning fire,

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: whereas the wicked will be in Hell;

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: And the Wicked - they will be in the Fire,

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: They will burn in it on the Day of Judgement,

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: They will enter it on the Day of Justice.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: roasting therein on the Day of Doom,

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: Roasted they shall be therein on the Day of Requital.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: In which they will enter, and taste its burning flame on the Day of Recompense,

15.author: Itani
15.text: They will enter it on the Day of Justice.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: They shall enter it on the Day of Recompense

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Therein they will enter, and taste its burning flame on the Day of Recompense,

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: They will burn therein on the Day of Judgment,

15.author: Qarai
15.text: entering it on the Day of Retribution,

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: roasting in it on the Day of Recompense

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: They will [enter to] burn therein on the Day of Recompense,

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: which they will enter on the Day of Judgment

15.author: Shakir
15.text: They shall enter it on the day of judgment.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: which they shall enter on the Day of Judgement,

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Which they will enter on the Day of Judgment,

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: And will not be removed from it.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And will not be able to hide from it.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: nor shall they ever be absent from it.

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And thence they shall not be allowed to be absent.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: And they (Al-Fujjar) will not be absent therefrom (i.e. will not go out from the Hell).

16.author: Itani
16.text: And they will not be absent from it.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: and then shall never come out of it.

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: And they will not be absent therefrom.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: And will not be absent thence.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: and they shall not be absent from it.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: and from it they shall never be absent.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And never therefrom will they be absent.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And they shall by no means be absent from it.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: and from which they will find no escape.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: And they will not be able to keep away therefrom.

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: How can you comprehend what the Day of Judgement is?

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: And what do you know – of what sort is the Day of Justice!

17.author: Arberry
17.text: And what shall teach thee what is the Day of Doom?

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: And what shall make thee know whatsoever the Day of Requital is?

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: And what will make you know what the Day of Recompense is?

17.author: Itani
17.text: But what will convey to you what the Day of Justice is?

17.author: Maududi
17.text: What do you know what the Day of Recompense is?

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: And what will make you know what the Day of Recompense is

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: Ah, what will convey unto thee what the Day of Judgment is!

17.author: Qarai
17.text: And what will show you what is the Day of Retribution?

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: What could let you know what the Day of Recompense is!

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: And what can make you know what is the Day of Recompense?

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: They will never be able to escape from it. Would that you knew what the Day of Judgment is!

17.author: Shakir
17.text: And what will make you realize what the day of judgment is?

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: What will make you realize what the Day of Judgement will be?

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: And what will explain to thee what the Day of Judgment is?

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: How then can you comprehend what the Day of Judgement is?

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: Again, what do you know – of what sort is the Day of Justice!

18.author: Arberry
18.text: Again, what shall teach thee what is the Day of Doom?

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Again, what shall make thee know whatsoever the Day of Requital is?

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: Again, what will make you know what the Day of Recompense is?

18.author: Itani
18.text: Then again, what will convey to you what the Day of Justice is?

18.author: Maududi
18.text: Again, what do you know what the Day of Recompense is?

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: Again, what will make you know what the Day of Recompense is

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Again, what will convey unto thee what the Day of Judgment is!

18.author: Qarai
18.text: Again, what will show you what is the Day of Retribution?

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: Again, what could let you know what the Day of Recompense is!

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: Then, what can make you know what is the Day of Recompense?

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: Again would that you only knew how terrible it really is!

18.author: Shakir
18.text: Again, what will make you realize what the day of judgment is?

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: Again: what will make you realize what the Day of Judgement will be?

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Again, what will explain to thee what the Day of Judgment is?

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Thumma ma adraka ma yawmu alddeeni

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: It is the day when no soul will have power to do the least for a soul, and God's alone will be done.

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: The day on which no soul will have the authority over any other soul; and on that day, the entire command belongs to Allah.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: A day when no soul shall possess aught to succour another soul; that day the Command shall belong unto God.

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: It is a Day whereon no soul shall own aught of power for any soul; and the command on that Day will be Allah's.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: (It will be) the Day when no person shall have power (to do) anything for another, and the Decision, that Day, will be (wholly) with Allah.

19.author: Itani
19.text: The Day when no soul will avail another soul anything; and the decision on that Day is God’s.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: It is the Day when no one shall have the power to do anything for another, and all command shall be Allah's.

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: (It will be) the Day when no person shall have power for another, and the Decision, that Day, will be with Allah.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: A day on which no soul hath power at all for any (other) soul. The (absolute) command on that day is Allah's.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: It is a day when no soul will be of any avail to another soul and all command that day will belong to Allah.

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: It is the Day when no soul can do a thing for another soul. That Day, the command belongs to Allah.

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: It is the Day when a soul will not possess for another soul [power to do] a thing; and the command, that Day, is [entirely] with Allah.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: On that day, no soul will be of any benefit to any other soul. On that day, all affairs will be in the hands of God.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: The day on which no soul shall control anything for (another) soul; and the command on that day shall be entirely Allah's.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: It will be a Day when no human being shall be of the least avail to any other human being, God [alone] will hold command on that Day.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: (It will be) the Day when no soul shall have power (to do) aught for another: For the command, that Day, will be (wholly) with Allah.

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Yawma la tamliku nafsun linafsin shayan waalamru yawmaithin lillahi
